notes: Bronze DQ rules for raw.anthropic_economic_index. Grain is composite (task_id, soc_code) with one NULL soc_code row for the task_name='none' placeholder. Thresholds calibrated to EDA findings at release_2025_03_27 (4,082 rows, global share sum=100.0 exact, automation axes sum=1.0 exact per row, 31.69% fully-filtered tasks). automation_pct and augmentation_pct are emitted in 0-100 percent units (matches observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in the project). Grain uniqueness treats NULL soc_code as a distinct value via COALESCE to '__NULL__'; this is safe because exactly one NULL is expected (task_name='none'). RAW-AEI-009 and RAW-AEI-012 were removed 2026-04-17 because they required the source fraction `filtered` column, which the ingestor intentionally does not persist (out of scope for this spec). The 6-axis sum invariant is already guaranteed inside the ingestor's _collapse_automation helper; DQ cannot reconstruct it without access to `filtered`. See governance/audit-trail/2026-04-17-dq-rule-writer-anthropic-economic-index-post-execution.md for rationale. RAW-AEI-019 added 2026-04-16 in response to chaos-monkey P1 finding that _normalize_onet_soc previously accepted dash-less SOC codes like '151252'; ingestor now validates via canonical ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ regex and the DQ rule is the defense-in-depth counterpart.

rules.name: Composite grain uniqueness: (task_id, soc_code)
rules.sql: SELECT task_id, COALESCE(soc_code, '__NULL__') AS soc_key, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index GROUP BY task_id, COALESCE(soc_code, '__NULL__') HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared Bronze grain is composite (task_id, soc_code). Every (task_id, soc_code) pair must be unique. NULL soc_code is coalesced to '__NULL__' so the one expected NULL row (task_name='none' placeholder) participates in the uniqueness check rather than being excluded. Duplicate pairs indicate an ingestor fan-out or dedup bug.
rules.rationale: EDA: '3,365 source tasks ... 82 tasks fan out to >=2 distinct SOCs (max 34-way). Total SOC-matched (task, SOC) pairs = 4,081. Total Bronze rows = 4,082 (+ 1 NULL placeholder).' Spec: 'Dedup grain: [task_id, soc_code] -- composite key.' NULL-handling choice (COALESCE to sentinel) picked because exactly 1 NULL is expected and we want to detect a future second NULL as a duplicate. Threshold: 0 duplicate pairs in ~4,082 rows.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 3,800-4,400
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 3800 OR cnt > 4400 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index)
rules.description: Bronze row count must fall in [3,800, 4,400] to reflect the expected 3,365 source tasks with documented multi-SOC fan-out. Count outside this band indicates a source-file change, a fan-out bug, or a placeholder-row regression.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Total Bronze rows = 4,082 (3,364 joined tasks + 82 fan-out adds to 4,081 task-SOC pairs + 1 placeholder row for task_name=none).' Spec: 'Tolerance band: 3,800-4,400 rows.' Threshold: 3800 <= count <= 4400.

rules.name: Global share invariant: SUM(task_pct) approximately 100
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ABS(SUM(task_pct) - 100.0) > 0.1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index
rules.description: task_pct is in percent units and represents a global share of classified Claude conversations. After even-split fan-out (raw_pct / N_soc_per_task), the sum across all Bronze rows must equal 100.0 within 0.1 tolerance. Violation means the fan-out split is wrong or the source file was altered.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'sum(pct) = 100.0000 across all 3,365 rows' in source; 'Splitting evenly by N ... conserves the 100% invariant' post-fan-out. Spec Bronze DQ: 'SUM(task_pct) ... approx 100 +/- 0.1'. Threshold: |SUM - 100| <= 0.1 (absolute, since source is 100.0000 exactly).

rules.name: task_pct range: 0 <= x <= 100
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE task_pct IS NOT NULL AND (task_pct < 0 OR task_pct > 100)
rules.description: task_pct is a percentage share. After fan-out split, per-row values must satisfy 0 <= x <= 100. Out-of-range values indicate a unit-conversion error (e.g. treating source pct as 0-1 instead of 0-100) or a negative-share corruption.
rules.rationale: EDA task_pct_v2.pct field profile: 'min 0.001541, max 6.650740, sum 100.0000'. After N-way split, max per-row value can only decrease. Spec Bronze DQ: 'task_pct range 0 <= x <= 100'. Threshold: 0 violations across all ~4,082 rows.

rules.name: task_statement non-null: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE task_statement IS NULL
rules.description: task_statement is the free-text task description and the semantic join key between Anthropic and O*NET. It must be populated for every Bronze row, including the 'none' placeholder row (where task_statement='none').
rules.rationale: EDA: Join key is 'task_name' (free text); 'task_name=none' placeholder row still has a non-null task_name. Spec schema: task_statement required=yes. Spec Bronze DQ: 'task_statement non-null 100%'. Threshold: 0 nulls in ~4,082 rows.

rules.name: task_id non-null: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE task_id IS NULL
rules.description: task_id is part of the composite grain and must be present on every row, including the 'none' placeholder. A null task_id makes the row un-joinable and breaks grain uniqueness.
rules.rationale: Spec schema: task_id required=yes. Spec: 'Grain: composite (task_id, soc_code).' A NULL task_id violates grain semantics. Threshold: 0 nulls in ~4,082 rows.

rules.name: soc_code non-null: >= 99% (exactly one NULL expected)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN null_count > 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN soc_code IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS null_count FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index)
rules.description: soc_code is NULL only for the task_name='none' placeholder row (exactly 1 expected NULL). Additional NULLs indicate a broken task->SOC join. Threshold is <= 5 NULLs to tolerate minor source drift (e.g. a handful of unmatched tasks on a future release) while still catching systemic join failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'After normalization, 3,364 / 3,365 Anthropic tasks match an O*NET task statement (99.97%). The one non-match is the literal placeholder none.' Spec: 'soc_code ... NULL only for the task_name=none placeholder row.' Spec Bronze DQ: 'soc_code non-null >=99% (exactly 1 null expected).' Using absolute count (<=5) rather than percentage for precision on a small-N expectation.

rules.name: soc_code format where non-null: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL AND NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: soc_code must be in XX-XXXX format after stripping the O*NET .NN detail suffix during ingest. A value like '15-1131.00', '151131', or empty string indicates the SOC normalization step in the ingestor failed.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'SOC format: XX-XXXX.NN (O*NET detailed). Stripping .NN yields 832 distinct base SOCs.' Domain context Anthropic section: 'O*NET-SOC codes ... Strip .NN to get base SOC XX-XXXX.' Consistent with RAW-KAI-006 pattern. Threshold: 0 malformed non-null SOC codes.

rules.name: Filtered rows emit NULL automation/augmentation
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE automation_pct IS NULL AND augmentation_pct IS NOT NULL UNION ALL SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE automation_pct IS NOT NULL AND augmentation_pct IS NULL
rules.description: Per spec, when filtered >= 0.999 the ingestor emits NULL for BOTH automation_pct and augmentation_pct (whole task is filtered, not meaningful to report). These two fields must therefore be either both NULL or both non-NULL -- never one-of-two.
rules.rationale: Spec implementation note 6: 'When filtered >= 0.999, both fields emit None.' EDA: '31.69% of tasks are fully filtered (filtered >= 0.999).' Expected null rate for automation_pct/augmentation_pct is approximately 31.7% of Bronze rows (higher given fan-out). This rule enforces the both-or-neither invariant; per-field null-rate bounds are handled elsewhere. Threshold: 0 mismatched rows.

rules.name: source_release non-null: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE source_release IS NULL
rules.description: source_release (e.g. 'release_2025_03_27') is the provenance stamp that pins the ingest to a specific HuggingFace release. Required because Anthropic publishes multiple releases with different schemas, and downstream refresh logic depends on knowing which release the Bronze table reflects.
rules.rationale: Spec schema: source_release required=yes. Domain context: 'Release pinning required ... Hard-pin release_2025_03_27 in the ingestor. Do not auto-follow HEAD.' Threshold: 0 nulls. Elevated to P1 (not P0) because it is a provenance column -- a bug here wouldn't corrupt the numeric data but would break reproducibility and release tracking.

rules.name: Outlier pct > 1 must map to knowledge-work SOCs
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE task_pct > 1.0 AND soc_code IS NOT NULL AND substr(soc_code, 1, 2) NOT IN ('15', '25', '27', '13', '11')
rules.description: The 8 Bronze rows with task_pct > 1.0 are documented in EDA as software-engineering / IT-support / technical-writing tasks. They must map to Computer (15-XXXX), Education (25-XXXX), Arts/Media (27-XXXX), Business (13-XXXX), or Management (11-XXXX) SOCs. A >1% task mapped to e.g. Transportation (53-XXXX) would indicate a join corruption or a major shift in Claude traffic distribution.
rules.rationale: EDA Anomalies: 'pct > 1.0 outlier 8 rows INFO Software/IT dominance. Consistent with Anthropic's published v2 report.' Domain context: 'pct > 1 outliers ... All are software-engineering / IT-support / technical-writing tasks. P1: verify all pct > 1 rows map to 15-XXXX (Computer), 25-3099 (Education), or 27-30XX (Media/Communications) SOCs.' Expanded the allow-list to include 13-XXXX and 11-XXXX as reasonable adjacents (e.g. technical writing can map to 13-XXXX business/analytical). Threshold: 0 violations -- fan-out on 8 source rows with up to a few SOC mappings each.

rules.name: source_url non-null and HuggingFace: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE source_url IS NULL OR source_url NOT LIKE 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Anthropic/EconomicIndex%'
rules.description: source_url must be populated and point to the Anthropic/EconomicIndex HuggingFace dataset. Required for CC-BY 4.0 attribution compliance.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'source_url required=yes, HuggingFace dataset URL.' CC-BY 4.0 compliance: 'Attribution ... https://huggingface.co/datasets/Anthropic/EconomicIndex'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: ingested_at and load_date freshness (within 30 days)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE ingested_at IS NULL OR load_date IS NULL OR load_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30' DAY LIMIT 1
rules.description: ingested_at and load_date are required provenance fields. load_date must be within 30 days of query execution during active pipeline builds. Since this is a pinned static snapshot, stale load_date past 30 days signals the Bronze table has not been refreshed after the initial land.
rules.rationale: Spec schema: ingested_at and load_date required=yes. Domain context: 'Snapshot. One release = one window of Claude traffic.' Consistent with RAW-KAI-017 pattern (30-day freshness for static snapshots). Threshold: 0 stale/null rows.

rules.name: source_method validity: hf_git_clone
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE source_method IS NULL OR source_method NOT IN ('hf_git_clone', 'local_cache')
rules.description: source_method must be 'hf_git_clone' (normal HuggingFace git clone path) or 'local_cache' (offline fallback). Any other value indicates an ingestor configuration error or an unauthorized code path.
rules.rationale: Spec schema: source_method required=yes, value 'hf_git_clone'. Spec offline fallback: 'Use cached files with warning log' (local_cache). Consistent with RAW-KAI-016 pattern. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Placeholder row preserved: exactly one task_name='none' row with NULL soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN placeholder_cnt <> 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS placeholder_cnt FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE task_statement = 'none' AND soc_code IS NULL)
rules.description: The source task_pct_v2.csv contains exactly one placeholder row with task_name='none' and pct=1.78 (1.78% of Claude traffic that couldn't be mapped to an O*NET task). Bronze must preserve this row verbatim with soc_code=NULL (no fan-out). Silver is responsible for filtering it. A 0-count or >1-count on this row indicates the ingestor dropped or duplicated the placeholder.
rules.rationale: EDA Edge Case #1: 'Placeholder task task_name=none in task_pct_v2.csv with pct=1.78. P0 rule: Bronze must preserve this row verbatim; Silver transformer MUST filter task_name=none before join.' Domain context edge cases table: 'Placeholder task_name=none with pct=1.78 ... Preserve in Bronze, filter in Silver.' Threshold: exactly 1 such row.

rules.name: soc_code matches canonical ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ or is NULL: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL AND NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every non-NULL soc_code value must match the canonical SOC regex '^\d{2}-\d{4}$' (two digits, dash, four digits). NULL is allowed only for the task_name='none' placeholder row (enforced separately by RAW-AEI-017). This rule catches malformed SOC codes that slipped past the ingestor's _normalize_onet_soc guard -- e.g. '151252' (no dash), '15-125' (too few digits), '15-12522' (too many digits), or garbled tokens with whitespace/letters. Added 2026-04-16 in response to chaos-monkey P1 finding that the ingestor previously accepted dash-less 6-digit strings as SOC codes.
rules.rationale: Chaos-monkey P1 Gap: '_normalize_onet_soc accepts malformed SOC codes (e.g. 151252 without dash)'. Ingestor fix (same commit) now validates shape with regex and returns None for malformed input. This DQ rule is the defense-in-depth counterpart that catches any future regression OR any row that was written before the ingestor fix. Overlaps intentionally with RAW-AEI-008 (same regex) but is kept separate and elevated to reflect the chaos-monkey source of the requirement. Threshold: 0 malformed non-null SOC codes.

rules.name: Fully-filtered task share: 25-38% of unique tasks
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN fully_filtered_pct < 0.25 OR fully_filtered_pct > 0.38 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN automation_pct IS NULL AND augmentation_pct IS NULL AND task_statement <> 'none' THEN task_id END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN task_statement <> 'none' THEN task_id END), 0) AS fully_filtered_pct FROM raw.anthropic_economic_index)
rules.description: 31.69% of Anthropic tasks have filtered >= 0.999 (fully filtered). A large shift in this rate (outside 25-38%) suggests the source classifier behavior changed or the ingestor's NULL-emission threshold drifted. Informational -- P2 tracks distributional drift.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'filtered >= 0.999 (fully filtered) 1,066 tasks 31.69%.' 25-38% window is a +/- 6 point band around 31.69% (roughly +/- 20% relative) to allow some release-over-release drift while catching large shifts. P2 because this is informational -- a drift here doesn't mean bad data, just a changed source characteristic. Threshold: count fraction in [0.25, 0.38].
